title: tulipa planifolia var. aximensis
intro: Tulipa planifolia var. aximensis, also known as the 'Bath Tulip', is a unique and rare species of the Tulipa genus, characterized by its single, cup-shaped flowers in shades of white or pale yellow. The medicinal properties of Tulipa planifolia var. aximensis include its potential use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, which may aid in the treatment of wounds and skin irritations. This plant is believed to have several health benefits, including its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote digestion. In herbal preparations, Tulipa planifolia var. aximensis is typically used in the form of a tincture or infusion, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
